Existing custom-image pipeline and shared galleries concentrated in East/West regions: the tenant has virtual machine image templates (16) and compute galleries/images with features like SecurityType and sources (20_04-lts-gen2) indicating custom image builds, so plan to reuse the image-template + Shared Image Gallery workflow to produce, harden, and distribute your OS images to the target regions for the 3‑tier VMs.

Widespread use of managed identities on VMs: 98.8% of virtual machines have SystemAssigned and UserAssigned identities, so integrate user- and system-assigned managed identities into VM provisioning and automation to enable credential-less hardening and backup orchestration.

Recovery and backup infrastructure already present with encryption and managed identities: Recovery Services vaults (90) show infrastructure encryption enabled and many vaults use user-assigned identities while backup vaults exist (18) with SystemAssigned identities, so schedule daily VM backups using these existing vaults, enable infrastructure encryption, and assign managed identities for backup jobs.

Tenant prefers Premium OS/data disks: a majority of disks are Premium_LRS and Premium tier (≈63% Premium_LRS, Premium tier ~68%), so select Premium_LRS (or equivalent) for OS and application disks to match performance/cost conventions and ensure backup/snapshot compatibility.

Scale sets and Standard networking SKUs are the normative pattern for horizontally scalable workloads: VirtualMachineScaleSets exist with UserAssigned identities and Standard SKUs while Load Balancers are Standard SKU and Application Gateways are WAF_v2, so implement the business-logic tier as VM Scale Sets behind a Standard Load Balancer or WAF_v2 Application Gateway to achieve application-level redundancy aligned with tenant practice.

Strong use of global DNS/traffic routing alongside regional load balancing: Traffic Manager profiles are very common (294) while Load Balancers are regional Standard SKU, so design multi-region frontends using Traffic Manager for DNS failover and regional Standard Load Balancer/Application Gateway pairs for local distribution and failover.

Network controls and private connectivity are used but many services still allow public access: there are large counts of Network Security Groups (957), Private Endpoints (56) and Private DNS Zones (74) while many key PaaS resources show publicNetworkAccess enabled, so plan to deploy backend tiers with private endpoints and NSGs and restrict public access for storage/keyvaults to harden the design.

Availability domain choices vary and availability sets show limited fault domains: availability sets use Aligned sku with 2 fault domains while VMSS entries show platformFaultDomainCount values including 5, so prefer VM Scale Sets across multiple fault domains or availability zones (where supported) rather than 2‑FD availability sets to improve availability of the business-logic tier.

Image build automation already uses user-assigned identities and customization scripts: image templates (16) are all using UserAssigned identities and include shell command customization steps, so incorporate the same image-template automation and user-assigned identities to bake hardened OS images (patching, antimalware, baseline config) and publish them to the gallery.

Application gateway fronting with WAF and HTTPS enforcement is an established pattern: Application Gateways are WAF_v2 with user-assigned identities and http->https redirect configured, so front the application tier with a WAF_v2 Application Gateway using a user-assigned identity for certificate/secret access and enforce HTTPS as part of the 3‑tier design.
